title: The New Day After the Sabbath
subtitle: Christ rises from the sealed tomb at the first light of morning

stillness: the rain is over and gone.
Be still, and let the bitter root of the cross fade away.
Wait in silence for the flower of life to break forth.
gospel: as it began to dawn toward the first day of the week,
For an angel of the Lord descended from heaven,
and by his power the earth was moved.
behold: Behold attentively, as if you were present.
See the soul of the Lord Jesus coming on the Lord's day,
with an honorable multitude of angels, to the tomb.
See him take up again that most holy body,
and by his own power rise,
and come forth from the tomb still sealed.
No one is so easily wakened and rises from sleep
as Christ rose from death and the grave;
for the Lord was roused as one who sleeps,
who says of himself: I slept, and I took my rest,
and I rose up, because the Lord upheld me.
As he came forth from the closed womb of the Virgin,
so he could come forth from the closed sepulchre.
consider: Rightly was Christ exalted by God to the glory of the Resurrection,
because for obedience to God the Father
he humbled himself even to the death of the cross.
He bore the mockings of the faithless,
the harshness of the scourges, the shame of the cross,
the bitterness of the gall, and at the last, death itself.
As Christ was humbled in this, that he bore evils by dying,
that he might free us from evils;
so he was glorified by rising,
that he might raise us up to good things.
He died for our offenses, and rose for our justification.
Human nature was bound fast by two deaths:
the death of the soul because of sin,
and the death of the flesh as the penalty of sin.
Because the Lord died in the flesh alone,
to free us from the death of soul and flesh,
he joined the light of his single death
to the darkness of our twofold death;
and by his single death he condemned our double death.
As the earth trembled when the Lord rose unto pardon,
how shall it tremble when he rises to the punishment of the guilty?
He did not delay his Resurrection beyond the third day,
lest he leave his disciples long tormented in sorrow.
His rising is the efficient cause of our own:
the first resurrection, which is our justification now;
the second, the raising of the body in the time to come.
No one will rise in the second resurrection to glory,
unless he has first risen in the first resurrection by grace.
prayer: who, the chains of death being broken, glorified your body,
and rose in glory so unspeakable:
I beg and beseech you, by your flowering Resurrection,
grant that I, rising from vices and from the death of the soul,
may ever flower in virtues, and walk in newness of life;
that I may seek and savor the things that are above,
not the things that are upon the earth.
By the power of your brightness,
cleanse my soul from the darkness of sins;
and by that same power, on the day of the universal resurrection,
let my flesh rise again to glory,
that with you I may rejoice eternally in both. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Radix amara crucis evanuit, flos vitæ cum fructibus erupit; et qui jacuit in morte, surrexit in gloria; sol post occasum oritur.
latinJewel.english: The bitter root of the cross has faded; the flower of life has burst forth with its fruits; and he who lay in death has risen in glory; the sun rises after its setting.
